route: 1 - BOUNDED RETURNS ON THE RANGE rho in [1,10]
target: c_k(q,rho) <= C*rho + 1, level-uniform, via c <= A(rho)*L(rho); A=return count, L=sojourn
verdict: CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA (same single-level refill/anti-stacking lemma as the aniso route)

measured_return_count_A_max_over_all_wal.level_stable: YES - A0 does NOT grow L5->L8 (flat <=5). This is the FLATTEST measured quantity (unlike max_c and sojourn L, which creep upward with level under extreme-value pressure).

measured_sojourn_L_max_arc_length_L8.rigorous_source: L(rho) <= 2*rho / c_diam = 2*rho/0.64 = 3.125*rho, from the PROVEN level-uniform diameter-ballistic bound S(n)=min_i cheb-diam(W[i..i+n]) >= 0.64*n (an arc staying within Cheb rho of q has diam <= 2rho => length <= 2rho/0.64).

anti_correlated_percenter_product_max(A*.note: A and L strongly anti-correlate: when A=5 the arcs are short (arclens e.g. [13,1,1,1,1] or [9,5,4,2,1]); the per-center product max_q A(q)L(q) is ~2.2x below A0*Lmax.

aniso_telescoping_rigorous_same_lemma.note: the birth-level telescoping (route3-aniso) delivers the cleanest rigorous constant, modulo the identical single-level refill lemma

direct_measured_max_c_over_rho.note: worst at rho=2 (lattice granularity); ~3.0-3.1 elsewhere; = the tight target C~2.5-3.5

how_the_bounded_range_is_used.finite_telescoping_depth: On rho<=10 the count/return recursion c_{k+1}(q,rho) <= c_k(M^-1 q,(4/9)rho)+refill contracts the radius x(4/9)<1 each level. After exactly J=4 levels the pulled-back inf-radius (4/9)^4*10 = 0.39 < 1/2 (also exact ||M^-4||inf*10 = 0.19 < 1/2), so the pulled-back ball holds <=1 lattice point => trivial base case A_base<=1. Thus A(rho) telescopes through only 4 M-levels, never rho>10: A(rho) <= A_base + sum_{j=0}^{3} tau_j, tau_j = fresh arcs created by level-(k-j) stitches.
how_the_bounded_range_is_used.returns_creep_is_outside_the_range: The prior returns-vs-sojourn stall (return count creeping 5->7->8) occurs at rho=20-30, OUTSIDE [1,10]; on rho<=10 the exact max return count is measured <=5 at every level L5..L8.
how_the_bounded_range_is_used.anchor_separation_rigorous: min_{v in Z^3, v!=0} |M v|_inf = 3 (verified exhaustively over |v|<=6, attained at v=(-1,-1,-1)). So the full anchor set M.Z^3 is Cheb->=3-separated, not merely consecutive anchors.

the_key_sublemma_left: SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL / ANTI-STACKING SLOPE: one level's fresh stitch interiors (short connector-word arcs, <=4 interiors/edge, decorating the >=3-separated anchor walk) have <= E*r points in ANY inf-ball of radius r, with a level-uniform constant E (measured E<=3, stable L6/L7/L8). Equivalently the per-level fresh-arc count tau_j <= tau0. This is the SAME irreducible 1-D arc-incidence fact the aniso and route3 routes reduce to; Route 1 reaches it from the returns side and CONFIRMS its level-stability (A0=5 flat through L8).

correction_to_the_route_premise.claim_examined: the task's proposed mechanism: 'each excursion is diameter-ballistic (diam>=0.64*len) and must re-enter B(q,rho<=10); bound how many times a ballistic path re-enters a fixed bounded ball'
correction_to_the_route_premise.finding: INSUFFICIENT ON ITS OWN. Diameter-ballistic S(n)>=0.64n LOWER-bounds excursion diameter, so long excursions are ALLOWED, not forbidden; it never limits recurrence. A path can ping-pong through a fixed ball unboundedly while every excursion has huge diameter (endpoint-ballistic FAILS, route3: nonadjacent Cheb-1 pairs). Diameter-ballistic bounds the SOJOURN L(rho)<=3.125*rho but CANNOT bound the return count A(rho). The genuine bound on A comes ONLY from M-renormalization (finite-depth telescoping above), which routes A back to the single-level refill lemma. This corrects why prior returns-based attempts stalled: they sought a ballistic re-entry bound that does not exist.
correction_to_the_route_premise.rigorous_unconditional_return_bound_is_c: The only lemma-free bound on nearby anchors is packing on the >=3-separated set M.Z^3: #anchors in B(q,r) <= ((2r/3)+1)^3, so tau_j and A(rho) are bounded by O(rho^3) unconditionally (useless, ~1000 at rho=4.44). Linear A0<=5 is metric/anti-stacking, not packing/arithmetic.

scripts: design/lemma/route1/measure_L8_arcs.py  (exact max A(rho), L(rho), max_c, per-center A*L over ALL walk centers, rho=1..10, L6/L7/L8; PYTHONPATH=. pypy3 measure_L8_arcs.py 8)
